verdict_summary: Three objections: (OBJ-1) material scope-enumeration error in M1 — the BA's 'All occurrences' list omits 5 active callsites; (OBJ-2) M1 rationale is internally contradictory — the BA states the line 615 short-circuit already precedes line 802 yet claims the insertion is necessary without identifying any scenario where 615 fires for the main agent but 804 is still reached; (OBJ-3) the BA Technical Hints for M2 contain an orphaned version of the signature-change description that specifies a three-argument call `_evaluate_push(command, sid, data)` but the recommended cleanest fix is `_evaluate_push(command, data)` with `sid` derived inside — the two descriptions contradict each other. AC1-AC6 and affected-file accuracy are confirmed correct.

objections.location: ticket-20260518-071337.md § Scope (Contract B) > M1 > All occurrences
objections.finding: The BA's scope enumeration under M1 lists only three occurrences of `check_and_consume_allowlist` in `pretool-bash-safety.sh`: line 615 (primary short-circuit), line 125 (comment), line 131 (comment). This is materially incomplete. The file contains five additional active call-sites at lines 983, 994, 1006, 1026, and 1109 — all of which invoke `check_and_consume_allowlist "$COMMAND" && exit 0` inside git-rule blocks (stash, checkout wide-path, restore wide-path, revert, and subagent revert respectively). These are NOT comment references; they are real defense-in-depth invocations already present in the file. The omission matters because: (1) it means the pattern the BA is asking dev to replicate already exists five times elsewhere in the same file at the same location within their respective blocks (immediately before the exit 2), confirming the pattern is well-established; and (2) it leaves the dev agent without the correct mental model of the existing structure.
objections.evidence: grep output: lines 983, 994, 1006, 1026, 1109 each contain `check_and_consume_allowlist "$COMMAND" && exit 0` as executable statements inside conditional blocks. Confirmed by direct file read.
objections.corrective_action: Update the 'All occurrences' list in Scope (Contract B) M1 to include all 7 active callsites: line 615 (global short-circuit), 983 (git stash push/save/create), 994 (git stash bare), 1006 (git checkout wide-path), 1026 (git restore wide-path), 1109 (git revert with modifier). The function definition at line 134 and comment references at lines 392, 434, 593, 693 should also be noted separately as non-call occurrences.

objections.location: ticket-20260518-071337.md § Context (Cause A) and § Evidence (M1 > Gap) and § Prior Attempts (Attempt 1 Failure category) and § Requirements M1
objections.finding: The BA's M1 rationale contains an internal contradiction that makes the stated purpose of the fix unclear. The BA confirms in three separate places that the line 615 short-circuit IS correctly positioned before line 802: (a) Context section Cause A says 'the short-circuit at line 615 should fire before lines 802-810'; (b) Evidence M1 Gap says 'The short-circuit is correctly positioned (line 615 precedes line 802)'; (c) Attempt 1 failure category is 'wrong_scope' not 'missing bypass'. Then the BA states the fix needed is to add a SECOND callsite inside the non-happy block at line ~804 as 'defense-in-depth for subagent-context edge cases'. But the Requirements M1 simultaneously says this defense-in-depth is 'explicitly out of scope — IS_SUBAGENT firewall in check_and_consume_allowlist remains intact'. This leaves zero identified scenarios where: the main agent reaches line 802 with an active /allow grant yet the line 615 callsite did NOT fire. The BA cannot articulate the failure case the fix is intended to address. Without a concrete failure scenario, the 'defense-in-depth' label is unfalsifiable and the AC1 test ('GIVEN pretool-bash-safety.sh modified per M1, WHEN main agent has active /allow grant') cannot distinguish whether the bypass would have worked already WITHOUT the M1 change (via the existing line 615 callsite), meaning AC1 does not actually validate M1's necessity.
objections.evidence: Direct quotes from ticket: 'The short-circuit is correctly positioned (line 615 precedes line 802)' (Evidence M1); 'defense-in-depth for subagent-context edge cases is explicitly out of scope' (Requirements M1); 'wrong_scope' as failure category for Attempt 1 (Prior Attempts). None of these three establish a scenario where 615 fires correctly AND 804 is subsequently reached for the main agent.
objections.corrective_action: The BA must identify a concrete, reproducible scenario where the main agent has an active /allow grant, executes `systemctl restart serve-trip.service`, line 615's `check_and_consume_allowlist` does NOT fire (or returns 1), and line 804's block fires. If no such scenario exists, M1 should be either (a) reconsidered as unnecessary because line 615 already covers the main agent, or (b) explicitly scoped as pure defense-in-depth with AC1 rewritten to test the existing line 615 path (not the new one) to avoid a circular test. If the real user-reported block is actually caused by the user issuing the command from a subagent context, then W1 (IS_SUBAGENT firewall preserved) means the fix does not help the user and the ticket should state this explicitly.

objections.finding: The M2 Technical Hints end with two mutually contradictory descriptions of the signature change. First the BA says 'Dev must change the signature of `_evaluate_push` to accept `data` as a second parameter and update the call site at line 663 to `_evaluate_push(command, sid, data)`.' Immediately after, the BA says 'The cleanest fix: change `_evaluate_push(command, sid)` signature to `_evaluate_push(command, data)`, derive `sid = _get_session_id(data)` inside the function... and update the call at line 663.' These two instructions conflict: the first keeps `sid` as a parameter (three-arg call) while the second removes it (two-arg call, deriving sid internally). The two-arg form is correct and consistent with all sibling evaluators (`_evaluate_reset_hard(command, data)`, `_evaluate_direct_ref_mutation(command, data)`, `_evaluate_forbidden_plumbing(command, data)`, `_evaluate_merge(command, data)`). The three-arg form does not exist in the codebase and would be inconsistent. A dev following the first instruction would produce a non-pattern-conformant function signature.
objections.evidence: File read confirms all sibling evaluators use `(command, data)` not `(command, sid, data)`. Call site at line 663 currently passes `sid` (not `data`), so the call site DOES need updating. The cleanest fix is correct; the first description is wrong.
objections.corrective_action: Remove or strike through the first description ('update the call site at line 663 to `_evaluate_push(command, sid, data)`'). The sole authoritative instruction should be: change signature to `_evaluate_push(command, data)`, add `sid = _get_session_id(data)` as the first line of the function body, update the call at line 663 to `_evaluate_push(command, data)`.

confirmed_correct.detail: check_and_consume_allowlist global short-circuit is confirmed at exactly line 615, BEFORE the non-happy systemctl block at lines 802-810 (confirmed by direct read).

Lines 802-810 confirmed: outer `if` tests for systemctl + !happy-daemon; inner `if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev` is where the exit 2 fires. Insertion point (immediately before `echo BLOCKED...` and `exit 2` inside the inner if) is precisely specified and correct.
Confirmed: DEV_SYSTEMD="" at line 48. check_systemctl_targets_all_dev with empty whitelist will return 1 for any service target, making the inner block fire unconditionally for any non-happy service.

Confirmed: check_and_consume_allowlist performs an inline fresh parse of $INPUT for agent_id at lines 141-147. Returns 1 for subagents regardless of call site. Adding a second call at line 804 does not bypass this.
Confirmed correct: if line 615 fires and consumes the grant, the grant is marked for PostToolUse deletion. The block at line 804 would not be reached. No double-consumption risk.
Confirmed: `def _evaluate_push(command, sid):` at line 571, function ends at line 595.
Confirmed: signature is `(command, sid)` — different from all other evaluators which use `(command, data)`.
Confirmed: no _check_git_allowlist call anywhere in _evaluate_push (lines 571-595).

confirmed_correct.item: M2 five sibling evaluators all have _check_git_allowlist
confirmed_correct.detail: Confirmed: _evaluate_commit line 455, _evaluate_merge line 480, _evaluate_reset_hard line 599, _evaluate_direct_ref_mutation line 612, _evaluate_forbidden_plumbing line 645.
Confirmed: `_evaluate_push(command, sid)` called at line 663 inside `_evaluate_command`.
Confirmed: at line 141 with signature `(command: str, data: dict) -> bool`. Takes `data` not `sid`. Calls `_get_session_id(data)` internally.
Testable: simulate /allow grant file, run hook with systemctl restart serve-trip.service, assert exit code 0.
Testable: no grant file, run hook with same command, assert exit code 2 and BLOCKED message.
Testable: simulate /allow grant, call _evaluate_push via unit test or integration test, assert returns without blocking.
Testable: no grant, no env var, assert _block_default_deny_push is called.
Testable: active /allow grant + `git push --force`, assert _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation fires at line 575 before _check_git_allowlist can run. Line 575 reference confirmed correct.
Testable as a code-structure assertion: read the function and verify the four checks appear in the stated order.
Correctly analyzed. _check_git_allowlist in the python file also checks data.get('agent_id') at line 146 and returns False for subagents, mirroring the bash-side firewall.
Correctly analyzed. _inline_env_present check at line 573 runs before any allowlist check and is an absolute ban.
Exactly hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh and hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — confirmed correct.
